taxonomy: Pentodon halei is classified as a species of beetle in the family Scarabaeidae, commonly known as scarabs. It belongs to the genus Pentodon, which is a group of beetles primarily found in Africa. Pentodon halei is further classified as a member of the order Coleoptera, which comprises beetles. The Scarabaeidae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of beetles, with over 30,000 known species. Pentodon halei, like other scarabs, has a distinctive body shape and is often found in warm and temperate regions.
